metadata.title: Ohrly for Automated Support
metadata.description: Discover when your bot, WhatsApp flow, or self-service journey starts resolving less before it becomes human queues, rework, or complaints.
metadata.ogTitle: Your bot is still working. But does it still resolve like before?
metadata.ogDescription: Ohrly shows when automated support flows start losing consistency before they become human queues.

hero.eyebrow: For CX, support, product, and automation consultants
hero.titleBefore: Your bot is still working. But does it still
hero.description: Ohrly shows when automated support flows start losing consistency, before it becomes human queues, rework, or complaints.

diagnosis.label: Ohrly Diagnosis
diagnosis.proofMetricValue: resolution by intent + handoff + repeat contact
diagnosis.reading: the flow is still active, but it started transferring more effort to the human team. Before redesigning everything, review the intent, fallback messages, and handoff points.

problem.title: When the queue grows, the problem may have started earlier.
problem.description: The bot may keep responding, but resolve less. The monthly report shows the numbers later. The team feels the impact first.

cards.title: Recurring fallback
cards.text: The bot responds, but finds fewer useful answers.

cards.title: Growing handoff
cards.text: More conversations leave self-service and fall to the human team.

cards.title: Unstable resolution
cards.text: The flow resolves on some days, but loses consistency on others.

cards.title: Repeat contact
cards.text: The customer comes back because the first attempt did not solve it.

cards.title: Pressed queue
cards.text: The human operation starts absorbing the degradation.

comparison.title: You already have metrics. What is missing is knowing when they became a decision.
rows: Whether handoff became a persistent pattern
What to monitor to prove improvement
comparison.calloutTitle: It is not another bot dashboard.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.description: A simple reading from signals you already have.

steps.title: Choose a flow
steps.text: Second copy, order status, billing, support, cancellation, onboarding, or another automated flow.

steps.title: Send basic data
steps.text: Sessions, intent, status, resolution, handoff, fallback, timestamps, and outcome.

steps.title: Ohrly identifies loss of consistency
steps.text: What changed, for how long, in which context, and with what operational pressure.

steps.title: Validate with the team
steps.text: The reading becomes an objective conversation: review intent, fallback, handoff, or human capacity.

steps.title: Track recurrence
steps.text: See whether the window came back, improved, or became a pattern.

example.label: Reading example
example.title: Second copy boleto flow via WhatsApp
example.paragraph1: Automated resolution fell for 4 consecutive days. In the same period, human handoff increased and average human handling time started to rise.
example.interpretation: the flow did not break, but it lost enough consistency to pressure the operation.
example.window: review fallback messages and handoff points for the “second copy” intent.
example.proof: automated resolution by intent + handoff by intent + repeat contact in 7 days.

audiences.label: Who it is for
audiences.title: Built for the people responsible when self-service gets worse.

items.title: CX and Support
items.text: Understand when the bot starts generating more pressure for the human team.

items.title: Product and Digital Channels
items.text: Validate whether flow changes maintained expected resolution.

items.title: Automation consultants
items.text: Show clients where the bot lost consistency and which adjustment to prioritize.

items.title: WhatsApp-first operations
items.text: Track critical flows before the problem becomes a queue or complaint.

return.title: Show return without promising miracles.
return.description: The reading connects evidence, decision, and operational impact without selling automatic root cause.
steps: The team sees where self-service lost resolution.
An action is taken on intent, fallback, handoff, or flow.
Resolution, handoff, repeat contact, or queue change after intervention.
Less human pressure, less rework, or a smaller queue.

notReplacement.title: Ohrly does not replace your bot platform.
notReplacement.description: It does not build flows, send messages, replace Blip, Zenvia, Zendesk, Intercom, Twilio, or Genesys, act as generic BI, or decide for the team.
notReplacement.calloutTitle: Ohrly is a reading layer over flow consistency.
notReplacement.calloutText: It shows when self-service started resolving less and where to look first.

contact.title: Want to know whether your bot still resolves like before?
contact.description: Send a support flow and receive a first reading on resolution, handoff, fallback, repeat contact, and pressure on the human operation.
contact.note: Start with a simple CSV or export. No mandatory integration for the first diagnosis.
contact.subject: New lead — Ohrly automated support LP
contact.name: Name
contact.message: Which flow do you want to analyze?
contact.messagePlaceholder: Ex.: second copy via WhatsApp, order status, billing, support, cancellation...
contact.privacy: By submitting, you authorize contact about the initial analysis. Do not send sensitive data through the form.
